review_text:
The staff are absolutely wonderful, the facilities are clean & tidy + the kitchenette & laundry were a handy bonus.
Thank you, Fay & Peter, for everything
Great place to stay great amenities very clean nothing was to much trouble for Fay and Peter and other staff highly recommended staying there
Cleanest amenities in any caravan park I've seen. Very welcoming, great two night stay, close to Australia Zoo 😄

We had a great six weeks here. The facilities are very clean … toilets, showers, laundry and kitchen. There is undercover seating and cooking facilities. Faye and Peter will go out of their way to help you with anything you need. Thank you for a great stay.
Didnt have a booking but managed to get us in the last slot. Very friendly, offering ample advice on what to do in the area. Cant fault facilities either
Having stayed for two nights. Very clean facilities everything you need. A late booking Fay the owner couldn’t do enough for us. Everyone friendly. Peaceful place.  Definitely recommend

The campsite is beautiful, very well maintained, and spotless. The hosts, Pete and his mother Fay, are incredibly friendly and welcoming. You can tell they really know what they’re doing and have a lot of experience. They take great care of everything and make sure guests feel completely comfortable. Truly one of the best places! Thank you so much! Cheers Rebecca and Joel

Nice little camp, very private wirh a big gate getting in and going out. Mostly are permanent campers but very friendly people, everyone was nice. Nice and tidy amenities. Only far from everything. Nice and quite place. We fou s it gets really warm in our campervan when sun is out because its in the open, not much shade.

We had such a lovely stay here and honestly didn’t want to leave. Fay and Pete are absolutely wonderful hosts so friendly welcoming and always up for a chat and always happy to help. They really go out of their way to make you feel at home. Everything was super clean and peaceful which made it so easy to relax and switch off. The whole place has such a calm friendly vibe we felt like family.
If I'm every back in the area would  definitely stay again

couldn’t recommend it more. 5 stars
Cracking little spot. Very friendly service and nice clean facilities. Close to Australia Zoo which was very handy for us. Would definitely stay again next time we are in the area.

Great location for visiting the Glasshouse Mountains and the Australia Zoo. It doesn't have a pool but it's Dog friendly and the amenities are spotlessly clean.
Fay,(Manager), does a great job and is very knowledgeable and helpful.
Nice van park tucked away off Steve Irwin way, just a few km out of Beerwah. Handy for zoo visits and staying around the hinterland area.  Stopped in here to catch up with family in the area.
Quiet Caravan park with lovely hosts, always trying to help their visitors. Amenities include shower, toilets, fully equipped kitchen, access to power and drinking water. Would definetly come back.

Such an amazing caravan site, the staff truly make the experience and it feels like such a community when you arrive. The site is extremely well maintained, with hot showers and spacious sites to park and 8 minutes away from Australia Zoo. I wouldn’t hesitate to stay again!

Sehr schöner und gepflegter Campingplatz, der wirklich alles bietet, was man für einen angenehmen Aufenthalt braucht. Besonders positiv aufgefallen sind uns die sehr sauberen und gut gepflegten Duschen und Toiletten, dass ist auf Campingplätzen nicht immer selbstverständlich. Man merkt, dass hier viel Wert auf Sauberkeit und Ordnung gelegt wird.

Wir waren zwar nur eine Nacht hier, hatten aber eine wirklich tolle Zeit und haben unseren Aufenthalt sehr genossen. Die Atmosphäre auf dem Platz ist ruhig, entspannt und sehr angenehm.

Ein ganz besonderes Dankeschön geht an Fay. Sie ist eine wunderbare Frau, unglaublich freundlich, hilfsbereit und mit einem grossen Herzen. Man fühlt sich sofort willkommen und gut aufgehoben. Ihre herzliche Art macht den Aufenthalt gleich noch schöner. Ich kann die ganzen negativen Kommentare gar nicht nachvollziehen.

Wir würden jederzeit wiederkommen und können diesen Campingplatz von Herzen weiterempfehlen.
What a gem. Faye and staff are super friendly and helpful. The facilities are spotless, we would definitely stay there again
Quiet clean spacious with new clean bathroom and shower facilities.

Ein kleiner sehr sauberer ruhiger, abgelegener Campingplatz. Die Toiletten und Duschen sind  sehr sauber.
Peter ist einfach nur nett freundlich und Hilfsbereit.
Man sollte wissen, dass es für Kinder auf diesem Platz keine speziellen Angebote gibt.

Wonder experience here, the guy who showed us around was lovely ! Bathroom was immaculate , electric hook up and water next to a concrete slab ! Lovely little gardens ! We was given a 10% discount card for Australia zoo ! Really reccomend this site !
Had such a good stay thanks to Fay's hospitality. She has the biggest heart, miss her already ❤️

Ripper spot near Australia zoo, with one of the most friendly operators i have met. Fay was welcoming and helpful, and the facilities were very well kept and clean. Will be staying again when driving past

Such a lovely quiet park. The amenities were so clean and tidy, one of the best we have ever seen. Peter, the host, a lovely guy and would help with anything. So close to Australia Zoo. A must to stay if u are in Beerwah!
Kerrie & Paul
Best bday ever  everyone sung happy bday to me and the owner nicest guy ever heard I thing and gave me a present in the morning definitely recommend this place 👍
Very clean, very welcoming. Would definitely recommend

This is an excellent no-frills independently owned caravan park. We would not hesitate to stay again and frankly wish we could have stayed longer although we visited fleetingly to visit Australia zoo (only 8 mins from this park!)

Fay is a wonderful host who met us at the gate and showed us to our site, great lady to have a chat with and she has excellent recommendations for local things to do. Nothing is too much bother and she even offered us an umbrella while we unhitched as it was pouring rain when we arrived.

The park has a high number of long-term residents, all of whom we encountered were very friendly and most have gone to effort to make their areas pretty with fairy lights, flower and herb gardens.

The sites are roomy and the ablutions were exceptionally clean and well maintained, probably the cleanest we have seen in a whole lap of Aus!!

Thank you Fay for having us!
Great place for stargazing. Fay was super awesome lady.. thank you so much Fay.

Good location to stay if travelling in motorhome or caravan if you are going to the Australia zoo, good pricing level and easy access to sites clean amenities and friendly staff... Thankyou to Fay for making us feel welcome...
It's clean and tidy, small space, a bit out of the way, full of residents. Alike other reviews, not the friendliest.. bit odd.

Camping gelegen  agrarisch gebied en stik bos waar veel vogels voorkomen. Rustige camping en ruime plekken voor de motorhome. Super nette en schone wc en douches. Zeer vriendelijke en behulpzame beheerster. Zeker een aanrader.

REALLY great caravan park! Just 5 mins out of Beerwah in the rural part, and close to Ustralia Zoo. The park was just SO NEAT, not squashed, large sites, and yhe amenities were so, so clean....never have I cone across a park yhat is so well kept, and maintained. The manager "Faye" was lovely, and nothing was too much trouble. Wrll done Faye! Both my wife and I loved staying in this very quiet park, and can thouroughly recommend it to anybody looking forward to enjoy a quality park and well cared for. Warren and Sue.

Love this Caravan Park, Fay n Peter are very lovely hosts.  Have stayed here a number of times, we needed to extend our stay due to an urgent matter and they were able to accommodate us. Thank you.  Cheers

really enjoyed our little stay! very cute and small caravan ! pete was very friendly and helpful
thank you
best caravan park we have stayed in . Ablution block the cleanest I have seen. Fay our host was a wealth of information and extremely helpful.

Best little caravan park I've been to yet. Sites are spacious. Pet friendly. Fay is an awesome host. There is a quaint area that provides free herbs. This camp is surrounded by strawberry 🍓 fields. Everyone is so friendly. The amenities are immaculate. It is a really quiet and tranquil spot. We will definitely be back.

What a wonderful place to stay. Fay was a pleasure to deal with and such a lovely lady. The caravan park has extremely clean amenities and you can tell the park is taken well care of.
A quiet place to spend time with your family and visit Australia Zoo in close proximity.
Thank you again and would definitely go back and stay there.

Fantastic little caravan park. The amenities are spotless and the staff and permanent residents are lovely people.
The park is in a beautiful quiet spot near town with Australia Zoo just down the road as well as the beautiful Glass House mountains. Highly recommend it. Catered for our 31ft motorhome easily. Having travelled from WA we have stayed at a lot of parks and this one rates in our top 3 for sure.😊

Quiet, Clean and friendly sums up the experience of this independently owned caravan park.

The park manager (Peter) is friendly and his big but super chill dog buddy welcomes all guests.

Most are permanent residents, so the caravan park has quite the character of different setups and artistic deco, friendly residents and I love the herb garden that they allow us to pick from - which I did for the rosemary to add on to my cooking.
Very welcoming and friendly park. Thanks Faye for your hospitality. Everything super clean and user friendly. Will be back. Cheers and enjoy your cruise Mal and Kerry👍🤗

This is a quiet, tranquil caravan park amongst strawberry fields, only a short drive from town. They have established a beautiful and unique garden with pavilion for guests to enjoy. You can help yourself to fresh herbs if you like.
The team at Beerwah Caravan Park is very friendly and tirelessly working on the cleanliness and upkeep of the facilities. The amenities are absolutely spotless. They have truly been outstanding on our travelling journey.
We can highly recommend this caravan park to the rest of the RV community.

L’accueil était sympathique et chaleureux, les propriétaires sont très agréables et l’ambiance est conviviale. Nous avons apprécié notre nuit au milieu des champs de fraises !

Mention spéciale pour la propreté du site qui est fabuleuse !

Merci !
Vivi et Liza

Les propriétaires sont très serviables
Des sanitaires très propres. On est au calme

Turned up looking for 2 nights  and they squeezed us in. When you first turn into this park you immediately notice how neat and tidy the place is, so much pride in this place.
Amenities are right in the middle of the park and wow what a great set up. Everything you need nothing you dont.
Cleanest Amenities i have come across to date such a credit to Faye and her team. Wonderful park
Good Park
Clean and tidy, quite a few permanents and all that i met were nice. Park is cheaper than closer to the coast, all in all a good stepping off point to explore the sunny coast and hinterland.
We were well looked after! Very friendly hosts and residents. Perfect for a quick trip to the Zoo.
Great people running it, friendly.
Very clean facilities. Manager was friendly and helpful. Easy-going place to stay for hiking in the Glasshouse Mountains.
Jan  , Pete and Staff are GREAT 👍 they do a fantastic Job and make your really Welcome

Quiet, friendly, very clean amenities. Insufficient lighting in park.
Overpriced.
Don’t be fooled by first appearance at the gate, it is the Cleanest caravan park I’ve been to, tidy and well run.  The Manager is friendly and helpful.

Edited this to add details and to put in some paragraphs.

Lovely place, an independent business tucked away in the middle of a working fruit farm. Nice people, excellent and clean facilities (showers, toilets, disabled ablution section too), and reasonably priced. A nice mix of long stayers and those passing through.

No website, you'll have to go old school and phone. The centre of the park are the showers and laundry, with a cooking or food prep area with microwaves, toaster oven, barbecue, hotplates and fridges. There are a range of condiments out for you to use, and it's all sparkling clean.

All these facilities are under cover, essential in Queensland, rain or shine. Lovely gardens you're welcome to pick herbs or what's in season from, and handing over your returnable bottles and cans to fund the garden is a nice way to give back.

There's a chemical toilet dump site (not under cover but flat access) and big bins for rubbish at that end of park too.

The working farm you drive through to get there is strawberries, lychees, pineapples and market veggies, but so far I haven't seen any sign of pick your own.

There is a fabulous-looking butcher down the road 100m, recommended by everyone I talk to), who does retail (and wholesale) sales. Not for the sensitive, as the cattle in the paddocks are the ones that end up in the butchery. They look happy and fat, no feed lots, just rich pastures.

You do see people picking the fruit, which must be a really hard job. Even when we visited in May (last month of Autumn), the sun was blazing and even locals were getting sunstroke.

The powered campsites are 10m X 13m, with a solid concrete pad on each one, lovely to have next to your van or tent for awnings and marquees. Water on tap too.

The quirky and friendly town of Beerwah (mix of tree changers, hippies, and  farming community locals), is maybe 5 minutes down the road, with a big Woolworths, an ALDI, and most things you need, including an excellent laundromat. There's also the train, if for some misguided reason you want to go to Brisbane, it's about an hour away.

Only about 25 mins to nearest Bunnings, there are several (on coast, or down Beerwah Forest road, or down M1 to Big Fish, or down to Morayfield). About 10 mins driving slowly to the Mitre10 at Glass House Mountains township but there's also a big hardware store in Beerwah, on right as you come into town along Simpson St, past the ALDI, and just past the turn for the Woolies, tucked behind the servo (petrol station) on right.

In Beerwah there's good free internet near or in the library, and in most places in town including the Beerwah Laundromat. At park it's sometimes okay, sometimes not, but better than our last stop in Glass House Mountains. Phone signal is excellent at the park for actual texts or calls.

All in all, a recommended stop, I've never felt so genuinely welcome. Thanks to Fay and Pete, and everyone else.

Stayed one night $50, during the Easter holiday craziness (every campsite was unhelpful) but Fay was extremely welcoming and made us feel at home. She spoke with us for a while and gave us some great suggestion for a sunset walk where she printed out a range of material for us to read about the areas.  showers and toilets are very clean and all other facilities were great! We would stay again and recommend to others
We spent four days here and found it very friendly and always neat and tidy facilities.
The facilities are very clean.

Wir kamen nach der Cyclon Alfred, leider im Dauerregen. Frau Fay war sehr nett und hilfsbereit.  Die Anlage ist sehr sauber und ordentlich.  Hat eine tolle Atmosphäre,  Sanitärbereich, Küche, Waschkühe, eine  große überdachte Gemeinschaftsraum. Sogar die Gasflaschen kann man hier nachfüllen. Einfach toll. Fr Fay gab uns noch eine 10% Bonuskarte für die Australia Zoo was wir auch eingelöst haben. Sehr empfehlenswert 👍👍👍

We arrived in the afternoon, the gate was closed, there was a phonenumber that we should call so we did that. To our disappointment, we were treated very rude over the phone, the lady bit our ear off and we felt she treated us like we were dumb..
We had a lovely stay at the park. The facilities are super clean and the residents were lovely. Faye was more than accommodating. Would recommend to fellow nomads with their pets.
Fantastic park with a very helpful manager Faye, quiet location and peaceful, highly recommend.  Thanks Faye for all your help.
A place very well cared for by the people in charge, clean, safe, and excellent place to rest.

We planned for two nights stay but ended up extending to almost two weeks!  Fay runs a tight ship, she is super organised and on the ball, the park is immaculate and everyone is friendly and helpful, a real little community.  The park is situated at a farm which makes it peaceful and private.  Sites are a decent size with adequate space between vans.  It is pet friendly and great for dogs.  Would definitely stay again! Thank you Fay!

We stayed at this caravan park for a week and it was just perfect. The people that run the park are so kind and friendly. The amenities and kitchen are regularly cleaned every single morning.
Pet friendly and really quiet places, definitely worth a stay.
Was meant to stay for two nights. Only stayed one barely. Was spoken to poorly by permanent residents which are pretty much the only people there.

Stayed for two nights with some friends over Christmas, and we were welcomed by Fay and Peter who were incredibly amenable. Would recommend this campsite for a relaxed stay, friendly atmosphere and excellent clean facilities.

We arrived very late due to some poor planning but Peter took our call and gave us a warm welcome at 9pm. I would totally recommend this campsite, it’s very clean, very quiet, you’ll get a good night of sleep for cheap. They also set up some nice Christmas lights!
Merry Christmas Peter!

Staff here are absolutely wonderful people.
They lent us some equipment we were lacking and even gifted some we required.
The park was lovely and tidy, neat and compact.
Very quiet.
Amenities were excellent. Always clean everything working well.
Washing machine extremely cheap compared to anywhere else.
Had the best four nights sleep here in this quiet location.
Excellent value for money.
Highly recommended to anyone wishing to stay in and around Beerwah
Only a short drive to Aldi and other shops.
This was my first stop as a grey nomad and am absolutely chuffed with the advice and help I received from the staff of this park. Thank you very very much.

Stayed here for a week and it was very enjoyable.  Sites are large and caravan park is away from highway noise.
The park manager is very helpful and friendly
The facilities are extremely clean and plentiful.
Don't go there,  Manament interfering in other people's personal information and takes one side and not a nice way to manage a park, don't trust very mean

This is a great little park with a huge camp kitchen and very clean amenities. Faye the Manager is very friendly and super helpful. Nothing was a problem and she's always happy to help. We even had access to the wonderful veggie garden.
Those giving it negative reviews clearly haven't done their research.
Yes, it's away from the town. Yes, it's mostly permanents (who are friendly and living a peaceful existence), and yes it's rustic. But there are no bogan campers drinking all night, no screaming kids on scooters and no schoolies in cabins.
If you are looking for pools,  jumping pillows and a place to party, this is not the place for you. If you want a safe, simple, clean an quiet place to make a base for a few days in the glasshouse mountains, then you'll really enjoy the park.
Super kitchen und Duschen Toilette perfekt

I would not recommend this caravan park as it is 90% permanent/ long term residents- very depressing place in the middle of nowhere.However it is well kept. Manager Fay was very defensive about her “ long stay “ residents , most of which seem to have been in residence for many years.

Perfect stay here for 6 nights. Fay is extremely helpful from when you arrive till you leave. Although mainly permanents we found our neighbours helpful with local info and general chit chat & always friendly. The whole park is well maintained, everywhere is clean & tidy and sites level. We used the App 'Pawshake' (doggy daycare) for our furbabies so we could visit the zoo, a first for us and now a service we will use again/ Perfect location for visiting the Glass Mountains and surrounding areas. Local butcher within walking distance of the park is definately a place to visit for your meats and local shops including woolies just a few minutes drive away/ Highly reccomend and will certainly be staying here next year. Thanks Fay - Philip, Chrissie, Brandy & Chelsea :-)

This park is clean and nice, amenities are close to each van/vehicle, Not a piece of grass out of place. Staff are lovely here!! It is a great gated RV park, powered and sullage sites available, clean amenities in both men’s and females!! Would recommend coming to stay here both permanently or just for a few days or weeks!!
Do you guys do long term. Powered sites?

This is a beautiful, well kept, clean and friendly site if you take it at face value which we were happy to do. We were charmed by the care and attention that went into the community areas and the cleanliness of the showers was top notch. There are quite a few residents here which adds a certain appeal and to its quirky character. Set amongst the strawberry fields, its an original and inviting camp. If you want the glitz of a chain campground, this probably isn't for you.
Lovely quiet place to stay. Bathrooms are beautifully tidy and clean. Good price for washing machine.

What a lovely, quiet park. Fay and her team of helpers do a wonderful job keeping the amenities clean and the grounds well maintained. Fay is a very caring and so helpful. I couldn't leave without giving her a hug. We need more people like her in this world.

We were greeted and welcomed by Fay, who then saw us to our site. The site was level and spacious. Power and water were easily accessible (no need for an extra hose or power cord).
This park is nestled amongst strawberry fields. It is quiet and peaceful. There are many permanent residents who have made a home here. Everyone was respectful and friendly.
It was easy to get to, about 7kms off the Bruce Highway.
I highly recommend this park for a stopover, or as a base to explore all that this area has to offer, and there is plenty!

What a great caravan park.So clean and tidy.
Would highly recommend this caravan park
Quiet and so laid back,willl return again.
Fay the manager was so nice and helpful
Lovely Fay so helpful! Clean and quiet. Can’t fault this place!
Had a great few days there.  Very peaceful and friendly.  Fay was very accomodating and lovely.  The cleanest caravan park we have been to.

Beautiful little caravan park hidden away from busy traffic, Peter was very helpful and the amenities were new and very clean, the sites are all powered and quite large and the grounds were well maintained

Clean toilets and showers, nice camp grounds. However, they clean the toilets at 7 am. when people are using it the most.
Washing machines have no hot water ( but no signage to tell you).  Fay seems nice enough, but a bit bossy.
At $50 a night, it's a bit high for where it is.
Sehr sauber, alles vorhanden was man auf einem Campingplatz erwartet. Nette Betreiber, bekamen sogar Rabattkarten für den Australia Zoo. Immer wieder gerne, wenn wir mal wieder hier sind.😏

Great clean place close to heaps of things has a good little kitchen and has good showers and toliets good to stay there if goin to aussie zoo or going to glass house or sea life and not far from beaches and faye is lovely and helpful

The host was very rude she kept raising her voice No Reason to talk like that. She got No Respect She’s A Grumpy Lady. A person with that Attitude should not be working in that position. But the Amenities & the Sites Are Very Nice Very Clean. I can recommend the Park But Not The Host
Fay is a fantastic host. The amenities and grounds are presented to a meticulous standard. We enjoyed our stay immensely and would not hesitate to go back again.

Nicht zu empfehlen. Dieser Caravan Park hat nicht mal einen Stern verdient. Die Gastgeberin / Inhaberin vor Ort war sehr unfreundlich und sagte uns, wenn ihr für weniger wie 50 Doller etwas anderes findet, dann geht doch einfach wieder. Wir fragten für eine unpowered site mit einem rooftop tent an. Jetzt sind wir 7 Kilometer zu einem anderen Campground (Ingenia) weitergefahren und zahlen da pro Nacht 36.- Dollar. Vielen Dank. 😊
A little gem of a caravan park. Conveniently located, spotlessly clean and very friendly.
Nice clean friendly place to stay

The Beerwah caravan park is the perfect location for the Glasshouse Mountains and the perfect location for the Australia Zoo. Friendly, tidy and the cleanest campsite we have been in Australia after a month travelling through Queensland. Highly recommended.

We were recieved friendly by the guy whose on in the evenings but in the morning the water was turned off when my partner  was having a shower. He was told he must be very dirty to have a shower that long. He was gone 15 minutes in total... When I went to ask what that was about  Fay the moning manager/housekeeper flipped at us. Said we were told the rules: 4 minute showers are the maximun! We weren't told anything and this rule was nowhere on display. Very nasty and unprofessional woman this Fay.
The most relaxing park I've ever been at

Toilets and showers cleaned daily, and spotless through-out the day anyway, caretaker faye is amazing to deal with aswell, stayed for 3 nights and didn't have any problems. If I'm up this way again I will be staying here
Small camp, friendly host, modern amenities & really close to Australia Zoo

Great little caravan park tucked in away from the hussel and bussel of big towns. Quiet, peaceful and very clean. Amenities are impeccable.
On arrival Faye met us at the gate,  showed us to our site and also offered us and later handed us local maps, and tourist information about Glasshouse Mountains and surrounding area's.
Thankyou for a pleasant stay,  we'll be sure to keep this little gem on our list for next time when we come back to this region.
Shayne & Grace
Delightful staff and a wonderfully quiet location… we will keep going back.
Nice and clean place close to Australia Zoo and Glass House Mountains. Fay is very Kind and gave all hinos about place and some regional point to checkout such as McCarthy's Lookout

Great clean park. Friendly owner.
Great amenities too
The amenities were impeccably clean. It was a lovely quiet park to stay at.
Spacious caravan sites, lovely clean amenities.  Lovely quiet and peaceful atmosphere.

Exceptional park. Friendly staff, clean facilities, good value @ $50 night powered site, free night per week. Large adequately spaced sites.
Very tidy and organised, grounds immaculately kept. Security coded gate for entry and exit. Thank you Fay and staff, this has been a wonderful place to stay and explore all that the Sunshine Coast has to offer. Barely more than half an hour from most attractions. We will be back 🥰
Very clean facilities and good location for exploring Beerwah, Glass House Mountains and Australia Zoo.
Highly recommended this park, very quiet. Management and Staff very helpful and friendly, amenities, laundry and camp kicken spotless. Very much enjoyed our stay thank you Fay and team🙂
Spotless amenities, very well maintained caravan park, lovely and quiet. Thanks Fay for making us feel welcome. We will go back.
One of the cleanest, tidies parks we've stayed in.

This is a lovely caravan park with the most immaculate ammenities I have ever experienced throughout our travels. Toilets and showers are spotless as well as the kitchen and laundry area. Would highly recommend. Spacious sites, gardens and very friendly permanents. Dog friendly, safe and good location. Thanks heaps Fay.
Great place to stay. Faye is a great manager and accommodating.  Exceptionally clean and tidy facilities.  Very secure park.

Stayed 3 nights in Beerwah Caravan Park and was amazed by it’s tranquil and cleanliness. The friendly caretaker, Fay gave a great introduction of Beerwah and gave excellent recommendations on where to venture. Love it! 😊👍🏼
